title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR SOYBEAN PRODUCTS FERMENTED WITH BACILLUS SPECIES Asia CXS 354R-2023 Adopted in 2023

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR SOYBEAN PRODUCTS FERMENTED WITH BACILLUS SPECIES Asia CXS 354R-2023 Adopted in 2023
sections.summary: Document title only: Regional Standard for Soybean Products Fermented with Bacillus Species (Asia CXS 354R-2023), adopted 2023.

Scope: applies to soybean products fermented with Bacillus spp. (as defined in Section 2) intended for direct consumption, catering, repacking or further processing, and explicitly excludes products covered by the Regional Standard for Fermented Soybean Paste (CXS 298R-2009).

Describes and classifies soybean products fermented with Bacillus spp. that retain bean shape and are not pastes, and defines three product types—Natto, Cheonggukjang and Thua Nao—each with distinct traditional processing steps, acceptable optional ingredients and final product forms (e.g., sticky/filamentous Natto; powder/paste/pellet Cheonggukjang; paste/dried Thua Nao).

Defines the product as soybeans fermented with Bacillus spp. (alone or with other microorganisms) that generally retain bean shape, are not pastes though some crushing may occur, and may be sticky and further processed into various product forms.

Classifies the standard’s products into three subtypes—Natto, Cheonggukjang and Thua Nao—each described by traditional processing (soaking, steaming/boiling, fermentation with Bacillus spp.), ingredient‑addition rules, and permitted product forms.

Natto: soybeans (including crushed) soaked and steamed then fermented with Bacillus subtilis var. natto with no materials added after fermentation; product must be sticky and filamentous with visible filaments when a bean is picked up.

Cheonggukjang: soybeans soaked then boiled/steamed/baked and fermented with naturally occurring or cultivated microorganisms (including Bacillus spp.); optional ingredients (per Section 3.1.2.2) may only be added after fermentation, and final forms may include powder, paste or spherical pellet.

Thua Nao: soybeans soaked and steamed or boiled, wrapped in broad leaves and fermented with Bacillus spp. (alone or with other microorganisms); optional ingredients (per Section 3.1.2.3) may be added, and final products may be paste or dried forms such as powder, sheet or pellet.

Sets composition, quality and sampling requirements: basic ingredients are soybeans, potable water and non‑pathogenic Bacillus spp.; lists optional ingredients by product subtype; requires characteristic flavour/odour/colour/texture and no visible foreign matter; sets component limits in Table 1 (Natto: moisture ≥53.0%, protein ≥10.0%, lipid ≥5.0; Cheonggukjang: moisture ≤58.0%, protein ≥12.5%, lipid ≥4.0; Thua Nao: moisture ≥53.0—or ≤15.0% if dried—and protein ≥10.0%), declares products failing these requirements defective, and sets lot acceptance by sampling plans with AQL 6.5.

Specifies basic composition: mandatory ingredients are soybeans, potable water and non‑pathogenic Bacillus spp.; lists optional ingredients for Natto, Cheonggukjang and Thua Nao (e.g., grains/flour, salt, seaweed, garlic, red pepper) with product‑specific allowances.

Lists basic ingredients for soybean products fermented with Bacillus spp.: soybeans, potable water, and Bacillus spp. (naturally occurring or cultivated), specified as non-pathogenic and non-toxin-producing.

Lists basic ingredients for soybean products fermented with Bacillus spp.: soybeans, potable water, and Bacillus spp. (naturally occurring or cultivated), specified as non-pathogenic and non-toxin-producing.

Specifies optional ingredients by product: Natto (grains/flour such as wheat, rice, barley; salt; seaweed/seaweed powder; other ingredients), Cheonggukjang (naturally occurring or cultivated microorganisms other than Bacillus spp., salt, garlic, red pepper powder, other ingredients), and Thua Nao (naturally occurring or cultivated microorganisms other than Bacillus spp., salt, other ingredients), with microorganisms noted as non-pathogenic and non-toxin-producing.

For Thua Nao, optional ingredients include naturally occurring or cultivated microorganisms other than Bacillus spp. (non-pathogenic and non-toxin-producing), salt, and other ingredients as appropriate.
Soybean products fermented with Bacillus spp. must have the characteristic flavour, odour, colour, and texture of the product and contain no visible foreign matter.

Table 1 sets numeric component requirements for moisture, protein and lipid for fermented soybean products: Natto—moisture ≥53.0%, protein ≥10.0%, lipid ≥5.0%; Cheonggukjang—moisture ≤58.0%, protein ≥12.5%, lipid ≥4.0%; Thua Nao—moisture ≥53.0% (for dried form moisture ≤15.0%), protein ≥10.0% and lipid not specified (values on wet weight basis).
Any product failing to meet the quality requirements in Sections 3.2 (sensory/foreign matter) or 3.3 (component requirements) is to be considered defective.

A lot meets the quality requirements of Section 3.2 when the number of defectives (per Section 3.4) does not exceed the acceptance number (c) from the appropriate sampling plans using an acceptable quality level (AQL) of 6.5.
Food additives are not permitted for the products covered by this standard.

Products must comply with maximum levels set by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits (MRLs) for pesticides.

Products should be prepared and handled in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex texts, and must comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Requires net weight to be indicated per the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and deems net weight requirements met when the average net weight of examined containers is not less than the declared weight provided there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.
Net weight of the products must be indicated in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).
Net weight requirements are met when the average net weight of all examined containers is not less than the declared weight, provided there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.

Products must be labelled per the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985); the product name should identify soybean products fermented with Bacillus spp. using the appropriate term in Section 2.2 (other non-misleading names allowed), and labelling of non-retail containers must follow CXS 346-2021.

The product name must identify the item as soybean products fermented with Bacillus spp., designated using the appropriate term in Section 2.2, with other names allowed under national law/custom provided they do not mislead consumers.
Labelling of non-retail containers must comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Compliance checks with this standard must use the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions being assessed.
